critical_finding: BreakerRegistry is entirely dead code — mcp-server.ts, ask.ts, wiki.ts all bypass it; the P0-6 circuit breaker never activates in production paths
day_4_must_fix: Wire BreakerRegistry into mcp-server.ts + ask.ts + wiki.ts (Sonnet+Codex 3/4 critical)
scheduler must call synthesizeWiki after reflect (Codex unique)
audit recordDecision wrapped in try/catch so failure does not roll back reflect (Opus unique)
half-open probe throws CircuitOpenError for non-probe concurrent callers instead of piggy-backing on probe response (Codex unique: response meant for prompt A would go to prompt B)
deferred: reflect.ts SQL missing ORDER BY (winner non-deterministic in rare ties)
audit INSERT+SELECT not atomic (use RETURNING) — bun:sqlite single process safe
wiki fallback no cooldown (every 6h retry flood)
ask.ts empty-hits + non-empty wiki fallback loses context
CircuitBreaker getState half-open vs locked reporting nuance
meta_lessons: Plan-audit locks the call-site inventory but doesn't verify wrappers are instantiated in production paths — grep 'new Ollama' + 'BreakerRegistry' next time
Scheduler call graph should be a plan-audit checklist item
Codex excels at SQL output ordering and concurrency semantics — 4/5 unique catches this round
Opus excels at architectural consistency but misses 'wiring completeness' — BreakerRegistry dead code is exactly this blindspot
